result.overall_plan: The goal is to build a 2x2 piston door that uses a lever to toggle four stone blocks between a floor-level position and an elevated position. The signal flow originates at a lever, travels across a redstone wire line, and powers four sticky pistons placed directly beneath the floor blocks. When the lever is active, the pistons extend, pushing the stone blocks upwards to clear the pathway.

construction_steps.reasoning: Place the first sticky piston at (2,0,0) facing upward. This acts as the primary actuator for the bottom-left doorway component. It is placed first to establish the anchor point for the door mechanism.
Place the second sticky piston at (2,0,1) facing upward to align with the bottom-right doorway component. This ensures symmetry in the door movement.
Place the third sticky piston at (3,0,0) facing upward. This is adjacent to the first piston, broadening the base of the door setup.
Place the fourth sticky piston at (3,0,1) facing upward to complete the 2x2 grid of actuators.
Place a stone block on top of the piston at (2,0,0). This block is the physical door component that will be moved by the piston.
Place a stone block on top of the piston at (2,0,1) to complete the left side of the door.
Place a stone block on top of the piston at (3,0,0) to start the right side of the door.
Place a stone block on top of the piston at (3,0,1) to complete the 2x2 door structure.
Place the lever at (-2,0,0). This serves as the input device to trigger the signal flow for the circuit.
Place redstone wire at (-1,0,0) to link the lever at (-2,0,0) to the transmission line.
Place redstone wire at (0,0,0) to continue the signal path toward the pistons.
Place redstone wire at (1,0,0) to complete the connection to the base of the pistons, ensuring the signal powers the entire array.
